data.title: Ethereum surpasses a $410 billion market cap as bulls get increasingly excited
data.description:
Ethereum ripped past a $410 billion market cap on Wednesday after jumping 5% to $3,400, its highest level since February, according to figures pulled from CoinGecko. The surge came as a wave of capital flowed into spot exchange-traded funds tied to the crypto. Nine ETH ETFs saw their largest-ever daily net inflow, totaling $726. 74 million, […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ethereum-surpasses-a-410-billion-market-cap/

He said the Federal Reserve needs a full-on “regime change” and slammed the people still running it, especially Jerome Powell, for what he sees as […]

data.title: OpenAI Introduces ChatGPT Agent
data.description: According to BlockBeats, OpenAI has announced the launch of its new ChatGPT agent. This development marks a significant step in the evolution of AI technology, offering enhanced capabilities for users seeking advanced conversational interactions. The ChatGPT agent is designed to improve user experience by providing more accurate and context-aware responses, further solidifying OpenAI's position in the AI industry.

data.title: White House Prepares for GENIUS Act Signing Ceremony
data.description: According to BlockBeats, crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett reported that the White House is preparing for the passage of the GENIUS Act today. The signing ceremony is scheduled for tomorrow at 2:30 PM Eastern Time, with industry leaders and legislators expected to attend.

data.title: Is Trump reshaping U.S.-China tech ties to strike a trade deal?
data.description:
Biden saw export controls as a “strategic asset” to keep the U. S. ahead of China in tech, but Trump is now scrapping that policy, signaling a major shift in high‑tech trade with Beijing. The administration informed Nvidia Corp. that its H20 AI accelerator, engineered for the Chinese market, could be sold there again in short […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/is-trump-reshaping-u-s-china-tech-ties-to-strike-a-trade-deal/

data.title: BlackRock's iShares Ethereum ETF Submits Staking Application
data.description: According to BlockBeats, market reports indicate that BlackRock's iShares Ethereum ETF has submitted a staking application. This development marks a significant step in the ETF's operations, potentially impacting the broader cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Coinbase to List Caldera (ERA)
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Coinbase announced that it will list Caldera (ERA) and support it as an ERC-20 token for transfer on the Ethereum network. The official reminds users not to send it through other networks, otherwise it may cause funds to be lost. 
Recharge is currently open, and trading will be opened after liquidity conditions are met. The ERA-USD trading pair will be launched in stages, and there may be restrictions in some regions. Caldera (ERA) will be labeled "Experimental".

data.title: TON Foundation launches native smart contract language

source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ton-launch-smart-contract-language/

data.title: DeFi Development Unveils International Treasury Platform Built on Solana
data.description:
TL;DR DeFi Development launches the “DFDV Treasury Accelerator” to build international treasury operations using Solana’s blockchain. The company will hold equity in each regional partner and deliver validator and staking infrastructure to strengthen the network. Solana’s real-world asset (RWA) value has surged 218% in 2025, while its token trades near crucial resistance levels, challenging Ethereum’s.
source: https://crypto-economy.com/defi-development-unveils-international-treasury-platform-built-on-solana/

data.title: U.S. Tariff Revenue Surges in First Half of 2025
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, U.S. government data reveals that tariff revenue soared to $87.2 billion in the first half of this year. The increase follows the implementation of reciprocal tariffs by U.S. President Donald Trump's administration in April. June alone saw tariff revenue reach $26.6 billion, quadrupling the usual amount. Analysis by the U.S. Customs and Border Protection indicates that the 10% baseline tariff has generated over $17.7 billion, while specific tariffs on the automotive sector have contributed more than $10.7 billion.

Estimates from Yale University's Budget Laboratory show that as of Sunday, the overall average effective tariff rate in the U.S. has reached 20.6%, the highest since 1910. Additionally, the proportion of tariffs in the total value of U.S. imports is rapidly increasing. A new round of reciprocal tariffs set to take effect on August 1 is expected to further elevate these figures.
